description_long: This object is a compact, stylized pagoda structure with a multi-tiered roof, each tier slightly smaller than the one below, creating a stepped appearance. The roofs are dark and appear to be constructed from tiles or shingles, with pronounced eaves that curve gently upward at the corners. The central body of the pagoda is solid and robust, with a square base and a prominent, decorative finial or spire at the apex. Flanking the main structure is a long, low, red-railed walkway or bridge, connecting to a smaller, secondary building or gate. The materials suggest a combination of stone, wood, and ceramic tiles, typical of traditional East Asian architecture. The overall form is symmetrical and ornamental, evoking the aesthetic of historical Chinese or Japanese pagodas often found in urban parks or temple complexes.
description: A compact, multi-tiered pagoda with upward-curving eaves, a decorative spire, and an attached red-railed walkway, evoking traditional East Asian architecture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the red-railed walkway and the layered pagoda roof structure.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the symmetrical, tiered roof and central spire of the pagoda.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the walkway and the stacked roof tiers from a different angle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the pagoda and the connection to the walkway.
